title: FAMILY VILLAGE SHELTER - 211info
text: PATH HOME FAMILY VILLAGE SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides 24/7 shelter in private, locked living and sleeping areas, meals, showers, laundry facilities and on-site supportive services and case management for families as they seek permanent housing. Eligibility: Families with children in their custody under the age of 18 (or plans for reunification) - OR - a third trimester pregnancy (28 weeks or 7 months) with connections to Multnomah County (i.e., was displaced, has kids in school, job, service providers in Multnomah County, etc.) AND meet one of the following criteria: -In an emergency shelter, transitional housing, motel paid for by an agency or voucher or a place not meant for habitation (i.e. car, streets, tent, abandoned building, etc.) -Doubled up (with family or friends and not on the lease, unsafely doubled up or in a living situation that is over safe capacity) and at risk of becoming literally homeless within 14 days -Housed, including self pay at a motel, and will become literally homeless within 14 days or has received a court ordered eviction notice -In an institution (i.e. jail, hospital, substance use or mental health treatment facility, etc.) and at risk of exiting to homelessness within 14 days -Fleeing or attempting to flee domestic violence and lacks the resources or support networks to obtain other safe, permanent housing HOUSEHOLDS ON A LEASE: Families who are currently on a lease and do not have an eviction notice are not eligible. Please seek rent payment assistance. YOUTH: Youth under 18 with children or pregnant are not eligible. Please seek assistance through the Coordinated Entry System for Youth. PETS: Pets allowed. The number and type of pets allowed vary by shelter. If you are offered a shelter space, the caseworker working with you will coordinate the shelter that best suits the family and their pets upon shelter placement. Hours: Callback List for Assessment: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Intake: scheduled by shelter provider after assessment when shelter space is available Shelter: 24/7 211 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone 211 Alternate (503) 222-5555 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call 211info to be added to 211’s Multnomah County’s Coordinated Entry for Families callback list to be assessed for program eligibility and enrollment. After being added to the callback list, a Homeless Services Specialist from 211 will call back within 48 hours to complete housing/shelter needs assessment. Please note this is not an immediate service and shelter placement could be a few weeks to months. When shelter becomes available, families will be called by a partnering agency and offered an intake appointment for shelter placement. All members of the household, including children, must be present during shelter intake. Family/friends/caseworkers can add individuals needing services to the callback list, however to complete the full assessment, the head of household will be contacted directly within a few days by a Homeless Services Specialist from 211. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Multnomah County Services: Community Shelters * Homeless Families Community Shelters * Pregnant Individuals If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. No Mapping Information Available
